title: x64dbg Introduction Lab
description: Attach to a simple process, set breakpoints, and inspect registers and memory.
steps: Download and install x64dbg from the official website
Open a simple target (e.g., notepad.exe or a crackme from crackmes.one)
Set a breakpoint on a WinAPI call (e.g., MessageBoxA)
Run the process and observe the breakpoint hit
Inspect RCX, RDX registers for arguments (x64 calling convention)
Step through instructions with F7/F8 and observe stack changes
Document your findings in the notes section
objectives: Attach a debugger to a running process
Read register values and stack memory

title: Cheat Engine Health Scan Lab
description: Find a player health value in AssaultCube using exact and changed-value scans.
steps: Download AssaultCube (open source FPS) from assault.cubers.net
Launch AssaultCube and attach Cheat Engine
Note your current health value in-game
Perform an exact value scan for health
Take damage and scan for decreased value
Repeat until 1-3 addresses remain
Verify by modifying the value and observing in-game change
objectives: Attach CE to a game process
Understand why multiple addresses may appear

title: Pointer Scan Lab
description: Create a stable pointer chain to a dynamic value that survives game restarts.
steps: Complete the health scan lab first to find a dynamic address
Right-click the address → Pointer scan for this address
Save the pointer map file
Restart the game and rescan pointers (filter changed paths)
Identify a stable pointer chain with module-relative base
Add a pointer entry in CE table and verify across restarts
objectives: Understand static base + offset pointer chains
Explain ASLR impact on absolute addresses

title: Assembly Reading Lab
description: Analyze a simple crackme using Ghidra and x64dbg together.
steps: Download a beginner crackme (level 1-2) from crackmes.one
Load in Ghidra, analyze, and locate the main/check function
Identify string references and comparison logic
Open in x64dbg and set breakpoint on the check function
Trace register values during comparison
Document the algorithm in pseudocode
objectives: Navigate Ghidra's disassembly and decompiler views

title: Memory Write Trace Lab
description: Use CE to find what code writes to a game value.
steps: Find a dynamic address (health/ammo) using scanning
Right-click → Find out what writes to this address
Perform the in-game action that changes the value
Analyze the writing instruction in the debugger
Trace back to the function responsible
Document the function's apparent purpose
objectives: Trace from effect to cause in code
Identify setter functions for game values

title: C++ Class Layout Lab
description: Reverse engineer a C++ class memory layout using ReClass.NET.
steps: Find a stable object pointer in a game (entity/player)
Attach ReClass.NET to the process
Create a new class at the object address
Identify vtable pointer at offset 0
Add fields and correlate with game state changes
Export the class definition and document padding
objectives: Use ReClass.NET for live structure editing

title: Network Packet Analysis Lab
description: Capture and analyze packets from an open-source multiplayer game.
steps: Set up an open-source multiplayer game server locally
Capture traffic with Wireshark during gameplay
Filter by UDP/TCP port used by the game
Identify repeating packet patterns for movement/actions
Correlate packet contents with game events
Document the packet header structure hypothesis
objectives: Capture game network traffic safely in a lab

title: PE File Analysis Lab
description: Analyze a game's PE structure to map imports, exports, and sections.
steps: Select a game executable (single-player, offline)
Open in PE-bear and examine DOS/NT headers
List all imported DLLs and categorize by purpose
Identify the entry point and code section size
Check for digital signatures and ASLR/DEP flags
Write a summary of the binary's characteristics
